# UC-20.1.6 · Cost Allocation by Team

## Description

Breaking down cloud costs by team/department via tagging creates accountability and enables chargeback/showback. Teams that see their own costs make better optimization decisions, driving organization-wide cost efficiency.

## Value

Breaking down cloud costs by team/department via tagging creates accountability and enables chargeback/showback. Teams that see their own costs make better optimization decisions, driving organization-wide cost efficiency.

## Implementation

Enforce tagging policy requiring Team/Department/Environment tags. Parse resource tags from billing data. Calculate cost allocation by team, department, and environment. Report on untagged resources (assign to "Unknown" for follow-up). Generate monthly chargeback reports.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk Add-on for AWS`, cloud billing TAs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CUR with tag data, organizational mapping.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enforce tagging policy requiring Team/Department/Environment tags. Parse resource tags from billing data. Calculate cost allocation by team, department, and environment. Report on untagged resources (assign to "Unknown" for follow-up). Generate monthly chargeback reports.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur"
| eval cost=tonumber(lineItem_UnblendedCost)
| eval team=coalesce('resourceTags_user_Team', 'resourceTags_user_team', "Untagged")
| stats sum(cost) as total_cost by team
| eventstats sum(total_cost) as grand_total
| eval cost_pct=round((total_cost/grand_total)*100, 1)
| sort -total_cost
| table team, total_cost, cost_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cost Allocation by Team** — Breaking down cloud costs by team/department via tagging creates accountability and enables chargeback/showback. Teams that see their own costs make better optimization decisions, driving organization-wide cost efficiency.

Documented **Data sources**: CUR with tag data, organizational mapping. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk Add-on for AWS`, cloud billing TAs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud_billing; **sourcetype**: aws:billing:cur. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud_billing, sourcetype="aws:billing:cur". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cost** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **team** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by team** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cost_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Cost Allocation by Team**): table team, total_cost, cost_pct

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Pie chart (cost by team), Bar chart (team costs with trending), Table (detailed allocation), Single value (untagged cost percentage).

## SPL

```spl
index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur"
| eval cost=tonumber(lineItem_UnblendedCost)
| eval team=coalesce('resourceTags_user_Team', 'resourceTags_user_team', "Untagged")
| stats sum(cost) as total_cost by team
| eventstats sum(total_cost) as grand_total
| eval cost_pct=round((total_cost/grand_total)*100, 1)
| sort -total_cost
| table team, total_cost, cost_pct
```

## Visualization

Pie chart (cost by team), Bar chart (team costs with trending), Table (detailed allocation), Single value (untagged cost percentage).
